protocol.name: lunaris_shell_overlay
protocol.description:
This protocol allows the Lunaris compositor to delegate rendering of shell overlay elements (context menus, tab bars, indicators) to the desktop-shell process.
The compositor acts as the server; the desktop-shell binds this global and receives overlay events. The desktop-shell sends back user actions.

interface.name: lunaris_shell_overlay_v1
interface.description: Singleton global. The desktop-shell binds this interface once at startup to receive overlay events from the compositor.

event.name: context_menu_begin
event.description: Sent before any context_menu_item or context_menu_separator events. x and y are global compositor coordinates where the menu should appear. menu_id uniquely identifies this menu instance.

arg.name: x
arg.summary: x position in compositor global coordinates

arg.name: y
arg.summary: y position in compositor global coordinates

event.name: context_menu_item
event.description:
Sent once per item after context_menu_begin, before context_menu_done. index is used when the shell sends activate back to the compositor. shortcut may be an empty string if no shortcut is defined. toggled and disabled are boolean (0 or 1).
Tree structure: parent_index is the index of the parent submenu item this entry belongs to, or 0xFFFFFFFF (u32::MAX) if the entry is top-level. label is the display string; when empty the shell derives it from action. has_submenu is 1 for entries that open a submenu (their action is ignored on activate); children of such an entry follow in the flat stream with parent_index set to this item's index.

arg.name: action
arg.summary: the action this item triggers (ignored if has_submenu=1)

arg.name: toggled
arg.summary: 1 if item is in toggled/checked state

arg.name: shortcut
arg.summary: keyboard shortcut label, may be empty

arg.name: parent_index
arg.summary: index of parent submenu item, or 0xFFFFFFFF if top-level

arg.name: label
arg.summary: display label; empty string means derive from action

arg.name: has_submenu
arg.summary: 1 if this item opens a submenu

event.name: context_menu_separator

description.summary: a separator in the context menu

event.description: Sent in place of context_menu_item for visual separators. index is incremented as normal so subsequent item indices remain stable. parent_index follows the same semantics as on context_menu_item.

arg.name: parent_index
arg.summary: index of parent submenu item, or 0xFFFFFFFF if top-level

event.name: context_menu_done

description.summary: end of context menu item sequence

event.description: Sent after all items have been sent. The shell should display the menu after receiving this event.

event.name: context_menu_closed
event.description: Sent when the compositor dismisses the menu (e.g. focus lost, window closed). The shell must hide the menu without sending activate or dismiss.

request.name: activate
request.description: Sent by the shell when the user clicks a menu item. The compositor looks up index in the pending callback list and invokes the corresponding action.

request.name: dismiss

description.summary: user dismissed the menu without activating an item

request.description: Sent when the user closes the menu without selecting anything.

event.name: tab_bar_show

description.summary: a window stack tab bar should be displayed

event.description: Sent when a window stack becomes visible or changes geometry. The shell should render a tab bar at the given position.

arg.name: x
arg.summary: x position in compositor global coordinates

arg.name: y
arg.summary: y position in compositor global coordinates

arg.name: width
arg.summary: tab bar width in logical pixels

arg.name: height
arg.summary: tab bar height in logical pixels

event.name: tab_bar_hide

description.summary: a window stack tab bar should be hidden

event.description: Sent when a window stack is destroyed or no longer visible.

event.name: tab_added

description.summary: a tab was added to a stack

event.description: Sent when a window is added to a stack. index is the position in the tab list. active is 1 if this tab is now the active tab.

arg.name: active
arg.summary: 1 if this tab is now active

event.name: tab_removed

description.summary: a tab was removed from a stack

event.description: Sent when a window is removed from a stack.

event.name: tab_activated

description.summary: the active tab in a stack changed

event.description: Sent when a different tab becomes active in a stack.

arg.name: index
arg.summary: index of the newly active tab

event.name: tab_title_changed
event.description: Sent when the title of a window in a stack changes.

request.name: tab_activate
request.description: Sent by the shell when the user clicks a tab in the tab bar.

arg.name: index
arg.summary: index of the tab to activate

event.name: indicator_show

description.summary: show or update a visual indicator

event.description: Sent when the compositor enters a mode that requires a visual indicator. Desktop-shell positions the indicator itself based on kind. edges and direction are only meaningful for kind=resize.

event.name: indicator_hide
event.description: Sent when the compositor leaves the mode that required the indicator.

entry.name: on_edge
entry.summary: viewport pans when cursor reaches edge

event.name: zoom_toolbar_show
event.description: Sent when accessibility zoom is activated. The shell should render a toolbar showing the current zoom level and controls.

event.name: zoom_toolbar_update
event.description: Sent when the zoom level changes while the toolbar is visible.

event.name: zoom_toolbar_hide
event.description: Sent when zoom is deactivated (level returns to 1.0).

description.summary: user pressed close on zoom toolbar
user selected a viewport movement mode
actions available on a window header bar

event.name: window_header_show
event.description:
Sent when a window with server-side decorations becomes visible. The shell should render a header bar at the given position.
If stack_id is non-zero the header belongs to a CosmicStack and the shell should render tabs inside the header (left of the title / window-control buttons). The shell correlates the stack's tabs via the existing `tab_added` / `tab_removed` / `tab_activated` events on that same stack_id. A stack_id value of 0 means "not a stack" and the shell renders the header with a simple title only.

arg.name: x
arg.summary: x position in compositor global coordinates

arg.name: y
arg.summary: y position in compositor global coordinates

arg.name: has_minimize
arg.summary: 1 if minimize button should be shown

arg.name: has_maximize
arg.summary: 1 if maximize button should be shown

arg.name: stack_id
arg.summary: stack id (0 = not a stack; >0 = render tabs for this stack)

event.name: window_header_update
event.description: Sent when a header bar's geometry, title, or activation state changes. `stack_id` keeps the same semantics as in `window_header_show`.

event.name: window_header_hide
event.description: Sent when the window is unmapped or switches to client-side decorations.

event.name: window_header_content

description.summary: titlebar content update from the lunaris-titlebar-v1 protocol

event.description: Sent when an application updates its declared titlebar content (tabs, buttons, breadcrumbs, search mode). The payload is a JSON object matching the TitlebarState schema. The shell uses this to render rich titlebar content for the given window.

event.name: window_drag_start

description.summary: interactive drag or resize just started

event.description: Sent once, at the moment the compositor installs a move or resize grab on the given window. During the drag the shell will receive a burst of `window_header_update` events at pointer-motion rate; this event lets it prepare — disable CSS transitions on that header, switch its Svelte store to synchronous writes, etc. Exactly one `window_drag_end` will follow, even if the grab ends abnormally (window closed, client crash, etc.).

arg.name: surface_id
arg.summary: surface identifier of the window being dragged

event.name: window_drag_end

description.summary: interactive drag or resize just ended

event.description: Sent once, as the pointer/touch grab releases. The shell restores its normal update path.

arg.name: surface_id
arg.summary: surface identifier of the window no longer being dragged

request.name: window_header_action

description.summary: user interacted with a header bar button

request.description: Sent by the shell when the user clicks a header bar button.

request.name: set_layout_mode

description.summary: set the layout mode for the active workspace

request.description: Sent by the shell to change the layout mode of the currently active workspace on the given output.

event.name: layout_mode_changed

description.summary: layout mode changed on a workspace

event.description: Sent when the layout mode changes (via keybinding, shell request, or workspace switch).

event.name: fullscreen_titlebar_reveal
event.description: Sent when the pointer reaches the top edge of the screen while a fullscreen window is active. The shell should animate the top bar sliding in (200ms). The bar must remain visible until fullscreen_titlebar_hide is received.

event.name: fullscreen_titlebar_hide
event.description: Sent after the pointer has left the titlebar area for 300ms. The shell should animate the top bar sliding out (200ms).

event.name: workspace_overlay_open
event.description: Sent when the user requests the workspace overview by keyboard (Super+Tab by default). The desktop-shell should open the overlay with keyboard focus on the currently active window. If the overlay is already open, the shell may use this event to cycle keyboard focus to the next window.

event.name: waypointer_open
event.description: Sent when the user taps the Super key (press and release without any other key in between). The desktop-shell should open the Waypointer overlay.

description.summary: window management actions for context menu items

entry.name: unstack
entry.summary: unstack this tab from its stack

entry.name: screenshot
entry.summary: take a screenshot of the window

entry.name: close_all
entry.summary: close all windows in a stack
